About: It is about the establishment of a unified Arab state through the rule of a Ba'athist vanguard party operating under a revolutionary socialist framework.

It is associated with Arab socialism.

CreatedBy: It was created by Michel Aflaq.
It was created by Zaki al-Arsuzi.
It was created by Salah al-Din al-Bitar.
CreatedTo: It was created to advocate the enlightenment of the Arabs.
It was created to advocate the renaissance of Arab culture, values and society.
It was created to advocate the creation of one-party states.
It was created to unite the Arab society.

It was developed by Salah al-Din al-Bitar.

It is characterized by the theories of Zaki al-Arsuzi.
It is characterized by the theories of Salah al-Din al-Bitar.
It is characterized by the rejection of Marxist class-struggle concept.
It is characterized by socialist economic policies.
It is characterized by state ownership of natural resources.
It is characterized by protectionism.
It is characterized by distribution of lands to peasants.
It is characterized by planned economies.
Influenced: It influenced Ba'athist Syria's state ideology.
It influenced the Iraqi Ba'ath Party.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Western socialist thinkers.
It was influenced by the French Revolution.
It was influenced by the German unification movements.
It was influenced by the Italian unification movements.
It was influenced by the Japanese economic miracle.
It was influenced by Georg Hegel.
It was influenced by Karl Marx.
It was influenced by Friedrich Nietzsche.
It was influenced by Oswald Spengler.
It was influenced by Sati' al-Husri's view that language was the principal defining and unifying factor of the Arab nation.
IsA: It is an Arab nationalist ideology.
It is a left-wing, Arab-centric ideology.
KnownFor: It is known for advocating the establishment of a unified Arab state.
It is known for advocating the rule of a Ba'athist vanguard party.
It is known for advocating revolutionary socialist framework.
It is known for advocating the enlightenment of the Arabs.
It is known for advocating the renaissance of Arab culture, values and society.
It is known for advocating the creation of one-party states.
It is known for advocating socialist economic policies.
It is known for its slogan Unity, Freedom, Socialism.
It is known for its opposition to Marxist class-struggle concept.
It is known for its historical opposition to affiliation with the American-led Western Bloc or the Soviet-led Eastern Bloc.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the interwar period.
It occurred during the Cold War.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the political thought of Syrian philosophers Michel Aflaq, Salah al-Din al-Bitar, and Zaki Arsuzi.
UsedAs: It is used as a vanguard party.
It is used as a basis for one-party states.
It is used as a tool for Arabization of ethnic minorities.
UsedFor: It is used for the establishment of a unified Arab state.
It is used for the rule of a Ba'athist vanguard party.
It is used for the renaissance of Arab culture, values and society.
It is used for the creation of one-party states.

It is used for ideological indoctrination.
It is used for the subordination of student organisations, trade unions, and other civil society institutions.
It is used for the legitimization of authoritarian rule.
UsedTo: It is used to advocate the establishment of a unified Arab state.
It is used to advocate the rule of a Ba'athist vanguard party.
It is used to advocate the renaissance of Arab culture, values and society.
It is used to advocate the creation of one-party states.
